key_insight: High-velocity air-fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and usable shape-memory behavior, which is rare for thermally sprayed NiTi.
engineering_relevance: This enables functional NiTi surface coatings for wear- and thermal-responsive components where conventional ceramic coatings cannot provide active actuation.
method: HVAF sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets.
Compared coating chemistry, porosity, microstructure, and phase transformation behavior.
Assessed functional response with bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests after annealing.
evidence_signal: The coatings were 100–300 μm thick, adhered well to steel, and showed martensitic transformation plus thermal actuation and shape-memory effects in mechanical tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings had inhomogeneous microstructure, oxides, voids, dislocations, and internal stress, so annealing is needed and tensile strength remains limited.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
